assertions.title: Hook script exists and is executable

steps.expected: Runs inject.py and outputs JSON with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext
steps.observed: SessionStart case runs inject.py --stdout --max-tokens 2000, captures output, wraps in JSON with hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName and hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext using Python json.dumps

assertions.title: SessionEnd handler runs ingest in background

steps.observed: SessionEnd case runs ingest.py --quiet with & and embed.py build with &. Comment notes 1.5s default timeout.

steps.expected: Runs ingest.py synchronously then distill.py in background
steps.observed: PreCompact case runs ingest.py --quiet synchronously (no &, with || true), then distill.py run --llm --limit 3 in background (with &)

assertions.title: Hook reads event from stdin JSON

steps.action: Inspect stdin reading code in hooks/memory-hook.sh
steps.expected: Reads stdin, extracts hook_event_name and source/reason/trigger fields
steps.observed: Script reads stdin via INPUT=$(cat), then uses Python json module to extract hook_event_name, source/reason/trigger (cascading), and session_id. Graceful fallback to empty string on errors.

steps.action: Read README.md and search for hook documentation
steps.expected: Contains 'Hook-Based Automation' section with settings.json config and SessionStart/SessionEnd/PreCompact explanation
steps.observed: README.md contains '### Hook-Based Automation (Recommended)' section with: full settings.json example (SessionStart/SessionEnd/PreCompact with timeouts), 'What each hook does' explanation for all three hooks, and note about hooks complementing cron

steps.expected: Hook calls inject.py --stdout; output wrapped in hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext JSON
steps.observed: Hook calls inject.py --stdout --max-tokens 2000. inject.py has --stdout flag (action='store_true') that prints output to stdout. Hook captures output and wraps it via Python json.dumps in hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed. All hook-related assertions verified through direct CLI execution (VAL-HOOK-001, VAL-HOOK-002) and code inspection (VAL-HOOK-003 through VAL-HOOK-006, VAL-DOC-003, VAL-CROSS-002). The hook script is well-structured with correct executable permissions, valid bash syntax, proper stdin JSON parsing, appropriate background/foreground execution patterns for each hook event, and complete README documentation.
